instructions:
Extend the local CI gate to also run Flutter doc-as-test as a third check (alongside the existing regex drift + TS doc-as-test). Mirror the design choices from 0051 (which wired TS doc-as-test). Also clean up the gitignore leak from 0052 that committed Flutter's generated .dart files.

## Part A — Gitignore cleanup

The Flutter framework's generated `.dart` files in `.docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/results/extracted/` got committed when 0052 landed because the gitignore pattern only covered the TS extracted directory. Fix:

1. Add to `.gitignore`:
   ```
   # Flutter doc-as-test generated files
   .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/results/extracted/*.dart
   .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/results/extracted/_manifest.json
   .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/.dart_tool/
   .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/pubspec.lock
   ```
2. Untrack the leaked files: `git rm -rf --cached .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/results/extracted/*.dart .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/.dart_tool .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/pubspec.lock`
3. Keep `.docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/results/extracted/.gitkeep` so the directory still exists in checkouts.

## Part B — Extend `check-drift.py` to run Flutter doc-as-test

Add a `run_flutter_doc_as_test_check()` function parallel to the existing `run_doc_as_test_check()` (which is TS-specific). Same shape:

  - Skip cleanly if `.docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/` doesn't exist (treat as unavailable, not as a failure).
  - Set `SP_SDKS_ROOT` env var when invoking the runner.
  - Run `python3 .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/extract-blocks.py` then `python3 .docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/run-tests.py`.
  - Read `.docs-ops/integration-tests/flutter/results/latest.json`.
  - **Gate policy for Flutter** (mirrors TS policy but with Dart severity classes):
    - `severity=error` errors → BLOCKING (real type/API drift in Dart land)
    - `severity=warning` warnings → WARNING (non-blocking)
    - `severity=info` / lints → ignore
  - Return: `{available, crashed, crash_reason, stats, blocking_failures, warning_failures}` (same schema as the TS function).

In `main()`, after the existing TS doc-as-test invocation, call the Flutter one. Combined gate verdict logic:

  - `regression OR ts_blocking_failures OR flutter_blocking_failures` → exit 1
  - else → exit 0

Update the printed summary to show all three signals on separate lines:

```
  Regex drift:           baseline=0  candidate=0  delta=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (TS):      passed=72  skipped=4  warnings=0  blocking=0  ✓
  Doc-as-test (Flutter): passed=41  skipped=3  warnings=0  blocking=0  ✓
  PASS — okay to push.
```

Keep the `--quiet` one-line output format extended too:

```
docs-ops drift: baseline=0 candidate=0 delta=0 new_pairs=0 ts_passed=72 ts_skipped=4 ts_blocking=0 fl_passed=41 fl_skipped=3 fl_blocking=0 verdict=PASS
```

Keep design symmetry — if you later add iOS / Android, the same pattern just needs another parallel function.

## Part C — Update CI_GATE.md

Update the Doc-as-test section to clarify:
  - Two languages currently covered: TypeScript + Flutter
  - Same gate policy applies per platform (blocking=type errors; warning=parser/severity-warning)
  - Per-platform scope: TS = 28 pages, Flutter = 29 pages (mostly same set + flutter-quick-start)
  - Future: iOS / Android coming

Also add a line in the existing 'Scope' subsection noting the TS-only constraint is now lifted.

## Verification

After the changes, run `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch` and verify:
  - Output shows ALL THREE signals (Regex, TS doc-as-test, Flutter doc-as-test)
  - Each is currently green (drift=0, TS pass+skip=100%, Flutter pass+skip=100%)
  - Exit code 0
  - The `--quiet` one-liner format shows both `ts_*` and `fl_*` fields

Report in `notes`: confirmation that gitignore leak is fixed (git status should show no .dart files modified after running the framework); output of a successful gate run; any edge cases (e.g., if `dart` CLI isn't installed, runner crash should be reported as 'unavailable', not as a blocking failure).

validators.name: gate_runs_all_three_checks_and_gitignore_clean
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate.out | tail -10 && grep -q 'Flutter' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'PASS' /tmp/gate.out && grep -q 'flutter/results/extracted/\*\.dart' .gitignore && echo OK
